page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 62.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The c4d-standard-16 instance offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources, aligning closely with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Because the virtual CPUs are dedicated rather than shared, the instance delivers predictable compute performance without the resource contention typical of burstable tiers. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces the baseline hardware complexity, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that rely entirely on network-attached storage and do not require hardware acceleration.

This server configuration is designed for workloads such as web serving, application hosting, containerized microservices, and moderate database operations. The dedicated 16 vCPUs and 62.0 GB of RAM support multi-threaded application environments, CI/CD pipelines, and general-purpose business applications that require stable CPU performance without massive memory footprints or specialized GPU processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 62.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.88 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for data persistence. With dedicated CPU allocation, the server avoids the performance fluctuations associated with shared-core environments, offering predictable execution for multi-threaded applications. The balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads that do not demand massive memory arrays or specialized hardware accelerators. It is suited for web serving, containerized microservices, application hosting, and general-purpose business workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16 is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 62 GB RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs, making it a reliable option for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-16 features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 62 GB RAM on x86_64 architecture, with no bundled local storage or GPUs.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance featuring sixteen virtual cores and balanced memory for predictable general-purpose and containerized workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated x86_64 virtual CPUs for predictable compute performance
62.0 GB of system memory providing 3.88 GB of RAM per core
No bundled local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Zero integrated GPU accelerators for standard non-accelerated workloads
Suited for web serving, containerized microservices, and application hosting
